roadmaps.title: Find easy Graph problems on LeetCode
roadmaps.roadmap:
**Roadmap: Finding Easy Graph Problems on LeetCode**

**Week 1-2: Graph Basics and Fundamentals**

* **Topics to Study:**
	+ Graph data structure: adjacency matrix, adjacency list, graph traversal (BFS, DFS)
	+ Graph operations: add vertex, add edge, remove vertex, remove edge
	+ Graph properties: cycle detection, connected components, graph diameter
* **Recommended Practice:**
	+ LeetCode Easy problems: Graph Traversal (1-3), Graph Operations (4-6)
	+ Practice graph traversal using a variety of graph representations
	+ Implement cycle detection and connected components algorithms
* **Revision Plan:**
	+ Review graph basics and fundamentals every day for 10-15 minutes
	+ Practice problems for 15-30 minutes, 3-4 times a week

**Week 3-4: Graph Algorithms**

* **Topics to Study:**
	+ Shortest Path Algorithms: Dijkstra's, Bellman-Ford, Floyd-Warshall
	+ Minimum Spanning Tree: Prim's, Kruskal's
	+ Topological Sort: Kahn's, DFS-based
* **Recommended Practice:**
	+ LeetCode Easy problems: Shortest Path (7-10), Minimum Spanning Tree (11-13), Topological Sort (14-16)
	+ Practice shortest path algorithms using various graph representations
	+ Implement minimum spanning tree and topological sort algorithms
* **Revision Plan:**
	+ Review graph algorithms every day for 10-15 minutes
	+ Practice problems for 15-30 minutes, 3-4 times a week

**Week 5-6: Advanced Graph Topics**

* **Topics to Study:**
	+ Network Flow: Ford-Fulkerson, Edmonds-Karp
	+ Matching Algorithms: Hopcroft-Karp, Blossom
	+ Planar Graphs: Euler's formula, planarity testing
* **Recommended Practice:**
	+ LeetCode Medium problems: Network Flow (17-20), Matching Algorithms (21-23), Planar Graphs (24-26)
	+ Practice network flow and matching algorithms using real-world examples
	+ Implement planar graph algorithms
* **Revision Plan:**
	+ Review advanced graph topics every day for 10-15 minutes
	+ Practice problems for 15-30 minutes, 3-4 times a week

**Week 7-8: Practice and Review**

* **Topics to Study:**
	+ Review all previous topics and practice problems
	+ Focus on weak areas and improve time complexity
* **Recommended Practice:**
	+ LeetCode Easy, Medium, and Hard problems: Graph Traversal (1-30), Graph Algorithms (31-60), Advanced Graph Topics (61-90)
	+ Practice problems for 30-60 minutes, 4-5 times a week
* **Revision Plan:**
	+ Review all previous topics and practice problems every day for 10-15 minutes
	+ Practice problems for 30-60 minutes, 4-5 times a week

**Interview Preparation Tips:**

* **Practice with Timed Problems:** Practice problems with a timer to simulate the interview experience.
* **Review Common Interview Questions:** Review common interview questions and practice answering them confidently.
* **Focus on Time Complexity:** Focus on improving time complexity for graph problems, as this is often a key aspect of graph-related interview questions.
* **Practice with Real-World Examples:** Practice graph problems with real-world examples to improve understanding and problem-solving skills.
* **Review and Revise:** Review and revise all previous topics and practice problems to ensure a solid foundation before the interview.

By following this roadmap, you'll be well-prepared to find and solve easy graph problems on LeetCode, and be confident in your ability to tackle graph-related interview questions.
